texto: The generative function is strictly nothing but an animal one, and can never be anything else. True spirituality demands its utter extirpation; and while its proper exercise for the continuation of the human race, in the semi-animal stage of its evolution, may not be considered sinful, its misuse, in any way, is fraught with the most terrible consequences physically, psychically and spiritually; and the forces connected with it are used for abnormal purposes only in the foulest practices of sorcery, the inevitable result of which is moral death—the annihilation of the individuality.
explicacao: Citação de James Morgan Pryse sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “The generative function is strictly nothing but an animal one, and can never be anything else. Tr…” articula fe, 1910, 22-1-5 em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como luto e 22-1-5.
